name: Aderyn
what_it_does: A Rust-based Solidity smart contract static analyzer that identifies vulnerabilities in blockchain code, supporting Foundry and Hardhat projects with zero-configuration setup and multiple report formats.
use_cases: Automated security scanning of Solidity smart contracts before deployment
Generating vulnerability reports in Markdown, JSON, or SARIF format for CI/CD pipelines
Integrating static analysis into VS Code or GitHub Actions for continuous security checks
not_for: Non-Solidity smart contract languages (Vyper, Rust/Anchor, etc.)
Runtime monitoring or dynamic analysis of deployed contracts
Full security audits (complements but does not replace manual review)
best_when: An agent needs to analyze Solidity smart contracts for known vulnerability patterns before deployment or during code review.
avoid_when: You are working with non-Solidity contracts or need dynamic/runtime analysis of deployed contracts.

auth.notes: Runs entirely locally as a CLI tool. No authentication required.

pricing.notes: GPL-3.0 licensed. Fully open source and free to use.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Tagged as mcp-server on GitHub but README contains no MCP-specific documentation, configuration examples, or tool definitions. MCP support appears to be either minimal or undocumented. Core CLI tool is well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Static analysis is inherently idempotent - same input produces same output.
known_agent_gotchas: MCP server capability is tagged but not documented - unclear what tools are exposed or how to configure it
Requires Foundry or Hardhat project structure - won't work on standalone .sol files without setup
Windows requires WSL - adds complexity for cross-platform agent deployments
GPL-3.0 license has copyleft implications for derivative works
Analysis scope limited to Solidity only
